meta.subject: Zambian Languages
meta.philosophy: The Zambian Languages Secondary Ordinary Level School Syllabus has been developed to facilitate learning that will promote competences in learners from Form 1 to 4. It has been designed to equip learners with skills to assess, criticize, analyse and practically apply knowledge that will help them gain life skills. The syllabus has been aligned with the Competence-Based Curriculum and 21st Century Skills.
meta.competence_learning: Competences to be developed include: Analytical Thinking, Citizenship, Collaboration, Communication, Creativity and Innovation, Critical Thinking, Emotional Intelligence, Environmental Sustainability, and Problem Solving.
goals: To equip learners with skills to assess, criticize, analyse and practically apply knowledge that will help them gain life skills.
To nurture effective communication both in speech and writing.
To ensure learners acquire knowledge, skills, techniques and positive values that are key in fostering total development of the individual, society and the nation as a whole.
To promote cultural preservation and increase learners' opportunities in fields such as translation, editing and publishing.

specific_outcomes: 2.1.1.1 Use appropriate language in discussions about national values and principles (patriotism, respect, hard work...)
2.1.2.1 Use appropriate language to debate with evidence (introducing motion, proposing, opposing, interjecting, rebutting...)
2.1.3.1 Use appropriate language to discuss cultural values (respect, hospitality, dressing, responsibility...)
2.1.4.1 Distinguish facts from opinions in conversations, speeches or meetings
2.2.1.1 Use appropriate language to act out plays on given cross-cutting themes (human rights, mental health, social and emotional learning...)
2.3.1.1 Analyse the meaning of contemporary and traditional songs

topics.topic_title: LISTENING AND SPEAKING - RIDDLES AND PUZZLES
specific_outcomes: 2.4.1.1 Analyse puzzles
2.5.1.1 Tell fiction stories
2.5.1.2 Analyse the moral lessons in fiction stories
2.6.1.1 Use appropriate language to deliver a speech on a given topic
2.7.1.1 Read a variety of Form 2 level texts with understanding
2.7.1.2 Summarise read texts
2.7.1.3 Paraphrase texts
2.8.1.1 Read Form 2 level texts with accuracy, pace and expression
2.9.1.1 Read a variety of texts
2.9.1.2 Summarise read texts orally and/or in writing
2.10.1.1 Locate information in books (dictionaries, magazines...)
2.10.2.1 Skim and scan different materials (magazines, newspapers, books...)
2.11.1.1 Write a biography of a given personality
2.11.2.1 Write expository composition
2.11.3.1 Write an application letter (job, services...)
2.11.4.1 Write a descriptive composition (places, objects)
2.11.5.1 Write a report about a given event
2.11.6.1 Write minutes of proceedings
2.11.7.1 Write an argumentative composition
2.12.1.1 Summarise given texts
2.13.1.1 Write dictated text
2.13.1.2 Write minutes of a recorded meeting (club, class meetings...)
2.13.2.1 Write articles for magazines and newspapers
2.14.1.1 Categorise nouns into noun classes
2.14.1.2 Analyse grammatical agreement in sentences
2.14.1.3 Construct sentences that express subject-object grammatical concord
2.15.1.1 Classify pronouns according to type (Possessive, Demonstrative...)
2.16.1.1 Use adjectives in sentences (number, colour, quality...)
2.17.1.1 Use different types of conjunctions in sentences (coordinating, correlative, contrastive...)
2.18.1.1 Use affixes to build words
2.18.2.1 Derive nouns from verbs and vice versa
2.19.1.1 Use interjections
2.20.1.1 Punctuate texts (capital letters, comma, full stop, exclamation mark, question mark...)
2.21.1.1 Use onomatopoeia
2.22.1.1 Use different forms of future tense
2.22.1.2 Construct positive and negative sentences in future tense
2.22.1.3 Construct interrogative sentences in the future tense

topics.topic_title: LANGUAGE STRUCTURE - ACTIVE AND PASSIVE VOICE
specific_outcomes: 2.23.1.1 Construct sentences in active and passive voice
2.23.1.2 Change sentences from active to passive voice and vice versa
2.24.1.1 Use different types of proverbs
2.24.2.1 Use sayings in sentences
2.25.1.1 Use Form 2 level words in sentences

2.26.1.1 Apply various translation techniques to translate text from English into a Zambian Language and vice versa (adaptation, amplification, compensation, particularisation, established equivalent, reduction)
